name: ds-toggle-switch
description: Binary on/off switch for settings-style boolean input, alternative to a checkbox.

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.

Contrast between the track and its border must clear AA in both contexts; this first registration ships with no api.events, so a full keyboard-activation probe is deferred to the revision that adds the archetype-specific activation contract.
First registration establishes the API hash; later drift is a rejection.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: modeled `checked`/`disabled` directly on ds-checkbox's existing boolean-property pattern rather than introducing a three-way state. Rejected alternative: an `indeterminate` third state mirroring some native tri-state checkbox implementations. Rejected because the requirement's stated states are exactly on/off/disabled, and an indeterminate toggle switch has no clear visual or semantic meaning for a binary settings control (unlike a checkbox summarizing a group) — adding an unused state would be an unchecked, unjustified API surface. Kept the token set intentionally close to ds-button's (--sem-color-action-bg/fg, --sem-color-focus-ring) instead of inventing a parallel 'switch' palette, since a toggle switch is functionally an action-colored control.
